tickets.title: US001: View course prerequisites
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to view the prerequisites for each course, so that I can plan my schedule without enrolling in the wrong courses.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Student can see the full prerequisite list for any course
- [ ] System displays a warning if a prerequisite has not been completed
- [ ] Prerequisite information is accurate and up to date

tickets.title: US002: Build a 4-year course roadmap
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to drag and drop courses into a calendar, so that I can visually plan my 4-year academic roadmap.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Student can drag and drop courses into semester slots
- [ ] System warns if a course is added to a full or unavailable slot
- [ ] Student can save and reload their roadmap at any time

tickets.title: US003: Receive early morning class warning
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to receive a warning when I select a class that starts at or before 9:00 AM, so that I can make an informed decision about my schedule.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] System displays a visible warning when a class starting at or before 9:00 AM is added
- [ ] Warning appears before the course is confirmed in the roadmap
- [ ] Student can still choose to keep the class after seeing the warning

tickets.title: US004: Manually override prerequisite requirements
tickets.description:
As an Administrator, I want to manually override prerequisite requirements for a student with special permission, so that I can support exceptional academic cases.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Administrator can select a student and override a specific prerequisite
- [ ] System logs every override with timestamp, advisor name, and reason
- [ ] Overridden courses are visually marked in the student's roadmap
- [ ] Override action is only available to authorized staff accounts

tickets.title: US005: Check real-time course availability
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to see real-time availability of courses, so that I can avoid adding full classes to my current semester registration.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] System displays current enrollment status for each course
- [ ] Full courses are clearly marked and cannot be added to current semester
- [ ] Availability updates automatically without requiring page refresh

tickets.title: NFR Epic 1: Security: No student should be able to view another student's schedule
tickets.description: Security: No student should be able to view another student's schedule or roadmap. All advisor prerequisite overrides must be logged for audit purposes.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 2: Performance: The system must sync with the live course database in rea
tickets.description: Performance: The system must sync with the live course database in real time so that seat availability is always up to date for current-semester registration.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 3: Usability: The interface must support drag-and-drop course planning an
tickets.description: Usability: The interface must support drag-and-drop course planning and display clear visual warnings, making it accessible and intuitive for first-year students.

analysis.ambiguity1:
Requirement: "The app should warn me if I am choosing a class that starts at 8:00 AM."

Why vague: The term "warn" is not defined — it is unclear whether the warning should be a popup, a color highlight, or a banner notification. Additionally, the threshold is ambiguous: does the warning apply only to 8:00 AM classes or to all early morning slots (e.g., before 9:00 AM)?
analysis.ambiguity2:
Requirement: "Students should be able to plan 4 years ahead, even if we do not know if the class is full yet."

Why vague: There is no defined behavior for how the system should handle future courses with unknown availability — should they appear as "Available", "Unknown", or be greyed out? No acceptance criteria exist for what "plan ahead" means in terms of system state.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict: The Registrar wants to block students from adding full classes to their roadmap. However, the Head of Advising wants students to plan 4 years ahead, including classes whose availability is unknown.

Solution: Allow students to add any course to their long-term roadmap (Year 2–4) regardless of availability, but display a warning badge such as "Full" or "Availability Unknown." Only enforce the enrollment block when a student attempts to register for a course in the current semester.
analysis.nfr1: Security: No student should be able to view another student's schedule or roadmap. All advisor prerequisite overrides must be logged for audit purposes.
analysis.nfr2: Performance: The system must sync with the live course database in real time so that seat availability is always up to date for current-semester registration.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: The interface must support drag-and-drop course planning and display clear visual warnings, making it accessible and intuitive for first-year students.
